Patch Set 2: Verified-1

Passed enforced test custom-1001 on RHEL 8.10 / x86_64 uploaded by Onyx Autotest from onyx-142vm1: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/529d6df5-cbec-4de0-91be-119596819c27 ran 3 tests. This build will be scored -1 since fortestonly was specified.

Patch Set 2:

FYI, https://review.whamcloud.com/58436 also ran a full sanity-sec test with master client and 2.15 servers. For 2.14 servers test 31 did not run since I did not comment out the check as you did in this patch. The 2.15 one was successful. 

As you presented in the ticket, I also think this was only fallout from 27a breaking. I've seen a similar issue in my own testing before in another context. 

I guess a follow-up task could be to clean up the nodemap and fileset environment in 27a properly, but this is probably not as straightforward as one would think due to ssk interactions and the way the nodemaps are setup/cleaned up in sanity-sec (disabling all traps, etc.). I don't believe this is a critical issue though.

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2:
> 
> FYI, https://review.whamcloud.com/58436 also ran a full sanity-sec test with master client and 2.15 servers. For 2.14 servers test 31 did not run since I did not comment out the check as you did in this patch. The 2.15 one was successful. 
> 
> As you presented in the ticket, I also think this was only fallout from 27a breaking. I've seen a similar issue in my own testing before in another context. 
> 
> I guess a follow-up task could be to clean up the nodemap and fileset environment in 27a properly, but this is probably not as straightforward as one would think due to ssk interactions and the way the nodemaps are setup/cleaned up in sanity-sec (disabling all traps, etc.). I don't believe this is a critical issue though.

Indeed, I will just push an equivalent of this patch on top of your b_es6_0 version of "LU-18357 test: fix sanity-sec 27a interop with long fileset" to be sure.
